title: 2023 DPA Education Impact Analysis
summary: Design education may not be the primary driver of salary outcomes; experience is more influential. Those with no design education earn 15.4% more than formally design-trained peers despite slightly less design experience. The published report states 23.6%, but its own median table (S$71,000 vs S$81,900) yields 15.4%; the derived figure is used here. 90.2% of non-traditional designers hold non-design tertiary education. Bachelor's degree holders earn S$82,400 median, Master's S$102,000. In-person certification correlates to 20% higher pay than having no design education. Larger companies and certain industries (Consumer Products) are more accommodating of non-traditional hires. Non-local companies pay 91.1% more for designers without design education. The published report states 91.5%, but its own median table (S$82,150 vs S$157,000) yields 91.1%; the derived figure is used here. International experience links to 99.1% more pay for this group.
methodology: Analysis of compensation outcomes by education type (formal design education, bootcamp, online certification, in-person certification, no design education), highest formal education level, and design education combinations. Deep-dive analysis on the 22.3% of respondents without formal design education, examining demographics, company profiles, seniority, and compensation factors.
